title: tetradenia hypophaea
intro: Tetradenia hypophaea, also known as the bitter bush or bitter leaf, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Lamiaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Tetradenia hypophaea include treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are typically harvested, dried, and then used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
